items.title: Scale before you power
items.avoid: Feeding raw features with large magnitude directly into `PolynomialFeatures`.
items.prefer: Wrap `StandardScaler` (or MinMaxScaler when appropriate) before polynomial expansion in a Pipeline.
items.why: Equalizing scale keeps x, x^2, ... on comparable numeric ranges so OLS and ridge remain stable.

items.title: Choose degree with out-of-sample evidence
items.avoid: Setting degree = number of training points minus one because it gives zero training error.
items.prefer: Grid-search degree with cross-validation; report both train and validation curves.
items.why: The goal is generalization, not interpolation of the training set.
items.example: Plot validation MSE vs degree; stop when validation error rises even if training MSE still falls.

items.title: Regularize when degree is high
items.avoid: Using vanilla OLS with degree 10+ on small datasets.
items.prefer: Add ridge (L2) or lasso (L1) after expansion, or cap degree and use domain knowledge.
items.why: High-degree OLS amplifies noise in high-power columns; shrinkage penalizes extreme coefficients.
items.example: Replace `LinearRegression()` with `Ridge(alpha=1.0)` in the pipeline when degree > 4 and n_samples < 100.
